issues.title: Inline issue creation form needs expandable/modal option for long descriptions
issues.description:
The current inline CreateIssueForm in board columns is too small for detailed issue descriptions and agent instructions. Users need a way to write longer, more detailed content.

Requirements:
- Add an optional way to expand the form or open it as a modal (whichever fits the existing UI patterns)
- Must stay consistent with the app design (slide-in panels, existing modal patterns)
- The expanded/modal view should support the same fields: title, description, priority, start workspace, plan mode, skip review
- The inline quick-add form stays as-is for simple issues � expansion is opt-in
- Consider a "expand" icon or "More" link in the inline form that transitions to the larger view

issues.title: Claude should use MCP or CLI tools instead of raw REST API for kanban operations
issues.description:
When Claude performs kanban operations (create issue, launch workspace, move issue, etc.) it currently calls the REST API directly via curl. This is fragile and bypasses the intended interfaces.

Goal: Claude should use the agentic-kanban MCP server tools or the CLI for all board operations.

Work items:
1. Audit what MCP tools are available (list_issues, create_issue, start_workspace, move_issue, etc.) and what the CLI supports
2. Identify gaps � operations Claude needs that aren't in MCP/CLI yet (e.g. create_issue + launch workspace in one step)
3. Add missing tools/commands so the full workflow is covered without falling back to REST
4. Ensure MCP tools and CLI reuse the same service layer (no duplication)
5. Update CLAUDE.md to instruct Claude to prefer MCP/CLI over REST for board ops

Consistency note: MCP server and CLI should share the same underlying service functions so adding a feature once covers both adapters.

issues.title: Parse and display Claude Code task_started events and TaskCreate/TaskUpdate tool calls in terminal output
issues.description:
The claude-output-parser currently only handles system/init, assistant, user, and result event types. Several important event types are dropped or shown as raw JSON.

## System events to parse
- system/task_started — background subagent spawned (has task_id, tool_use_id, description, task_type)
- system/notification — system notifications (key, text, priority)

## Tool calls to render specially
- TaskCreate — Claude creates a todo item (subject, description, activeForm)
- TaskUpdate — Claude updates a todo item (taskId, status, subject, description)
- TaskList / TaskGet — Claude reads task state

## Actual event data examples

### system/task_started
```json
{"type":"system","subtype":"task_started","task_id":"b2fkfrvnz","tool_use_id":"toolu_bdrk_0147VmJccFhojDx6FM3wRrzJ","description":"Test CLI from main checkout","task_type":"local_bash"}
```

### TaskCreate tool_use (inside assistant message content)
Input: {"subject":"Fix parsing bug","description":"Update parser to handle new events","activeForm":"Fixing parsing bug"}

### TaskUpdate tool_use (inside assistant message content)
Input: {"taskId":"abc123","status":"completed"}

## Implementation
- Add new ParsedEvent types for task_started and notification system events
- Add special rendering for TaskCreate/TaskUpdate/TaskGet/TaskList tool calls (show as task list progress)
- Both minimal and full parse modes should display these nicely

issues.title: Show agent configuration on issue cards
issues.description:
Issue cards should display the agent configuration (profile name like zai/anth/gateway, etc.) that is used for the current agent session. This makes it easy to see at a glance which configuration each workspace is running with.

Implementation:

- Include profile in board API workspace summary
- Display as a small badge on IssueCard near the workspace status line

issues.title: Enhance mock agent to support testing complex agent workflows
issues.description:
The current mock agent (`MOCK_AGENT=1`) simulates basic agent output but does not cover multi-turn conversations, streaming output parsing, session resume chains (`--resume`), or error scenarios. This limits E2E test coverage for the most critical agent-interactive features.

**Scope:**
- Extend the mock agent (in `packages/server/src/services/`) to support configurable behavior profiles (e.g., normal completion, multi-turn with follow-up prompts, error-on-launch, slow-streaming output, session resume responses)
- Ensure it emits properly formatted stream-json events (system/init with `session_id`, assistant messages, result events) so that `session.manager.ts` broadcast parsing works identically to a real Claude agent
- Support `--resume` flag passthrough so the resume chain logic can be tested end-to-end
- Add a way to simulate `claudeSessionId` capture from `system/init` events so session resume lookups succeed

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Mock agent can be configured to produce multi-turn conversations (agent responds, waits for stdin, responds again)
- [ ] Mock agent emits `system/init` with a deterministic `session_id` that gets stored in `sessions.claudeSessionId`
- [ ] E2E tests can cover session resume (launch → exit → relaunch with `--resume`) using the mock agent
- [ ] Mock agent supports a configurable delay profile for streaming output
- [ ] Existing E2E and unit tests continue to pass with `MOCK_AGENT=1`

**Agent instructions:**
- Start by reading the current mock agent implementation and the real agent's stream-json output format
- Keep changes localized to the mock agent module and test files — do not modify production agent launch logic
- Run `pnpm test:e2e` to verify no regressions

issues.title: Show diff stats on issue cards for at-a-glance change tracking
issues.description:
Display concise git diff information directly on kanban issue cards so you can quickly see which tickets have significant changes without opening each one.

**Context**
Currently, to see what changed on a ticket you must open the workspace panel and view the full diff. This makes it hard to scan the board and identify tickets with large unreviewed changes, stuck work, or merge-ready branches.

**Acceptance criteria**
- Each issue card with an active or closed workspace shows a compact diff summary (files changed, insertions, deletions) using the existing `workspaceSummary` data from the board API.
- Diff stats render as a small inline element (e.g. `+42 / -7 · 5 files`) with color-coded additions (green) and deletions (red).
- Cards with no workspace or no changes show nothing (no empty placeholder).
- The summary updates in real-time via the existing WebSocket board refresh.
- Works for both worktree and direct workspaces.

**Implementation notes**
- The board endpoint already enriches issues with `workspaceSummary`. Check whether diff stats are included or need to be added to the aggregation query.
- Keep the display minimal — this is a card-level indicator, not a full diff viewer.
- Consider showing the diff only when the workspace status is `active` or `closed` (not `idle` before any work starts).

issues.title: Show active subagents and task progress on issue cards
issues.description:
## Problem
Issue cards currently show live session stats (model name, token count) but give no visibility into what the agent is actually *doing*. Users must open the workspace panel and read terminal output to understand progress.

## Proposal
Extend the real-time WebSocket session stats to include two new data points surfaced on each issue card:

### 1. Active Subagents
- Detect when the agent spawns subagents (look for `Agent` tool calls in the streaming JSON output)
- Show a small badge or indicator on the card: e.g. "2 subagents running"
- Optional: list subagent descriptions (truncated) on hover or in a popover

### 2. Task / Todo Progress
- Detect `TaskCreate` / `TaskUpdate` tool calls in the agent's streaming output
- Track task list state (subject + status) per session
- Show a compact progress bar or fraction on the card: e.g. "3/5 tasks done"
- Optional: expandable list of task names with status indicators (pending/in_progress/completed)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Agent streaming output is parsed for `Agent`, `TaskCreate`, and `TaskUpdate` tool-use events
- [ ] Parsed state is broadcast via the existing WebSocket session-stats channel
- [ ] Issue cards display subagent count when ≥1 is active
- [ ] Issue cards display task progress (completed/total) when tasks exist
- [ ] Data clears/reset when the session ends
- [ ] No performance regression on board rendering with many active sessions

## Agent Instructions
- Extend the streaming output parser in `session.manager.ts` (where `system/init` events are already parsed for model/token info) to also capture `Agent` and `TaskCreate`/`TaskUpdate` tool calls
- Store per-session subagent/task state in memory (no DB schema changes needed — this is ephemeral)
- Extend the WebSocket broadcast payload for session stats to include `subagents` and `tasks` fields
- Update `IssueCard.tsx` to render the new indicators using the existing `workspaceSummary` data flow
- Keep the UI minimal — badges and a tiny progress bar, not full lists (those belong in the workspace panel)

issues.title: code review in the workflow doesnt get properly triggered and as part of the ai review, the base branch that was used for branching should be merged/rebased to compare with what really changed on the worktree
the agentic kanban app should leverage agent skills properly and make them adjustable and useable depending on project
issues.description: currently the user has no control about what prompt is used for the code review, or modify the workflow that interacts with the kanban app. the agent skill standard (and especially claude code support for it) give a nice structure for reusable and adjustable prompts and workflows. ideally the user should be able to open an arbitrary project in our app and use a claude code instance in his terminal to interact with the kanban board for his project. for this it would be helpful to have a mechanism to copy the prompts to the proper claude code skills folder or symlink them or have other mechanisms to allow reuse with a coding agent outside the webapp
